content:
Not that long ago China was 6-to-8 months behind the US in frontier AI models. And now it seems that they finally caught up, in the worst possible moment.
I’ll tell you what I think the “kimi-moment” is, and why I’m not optimistic about the future, despite this being a very good thing. But first let’s talk accusations.
You stole our weights
What a moral dillema! If you steal something that’s already stolen, who has the moral high ground? Chinese models are often accused of being trained ON the American models, basically “stealing” their secret source without the multi-Billion dollar investment in training and infrastructure.
While the cost to effort ratio is definitely real, most of the companies crying outrage purposefully leave out one tiny part:
American models trained on copyrighter work without permission. When asked about some of my design methods, big AI models repeat my own words back to me. Yeah, I know.
It reminds me of this meme, but they’re super-serious about it to a point where it’s pretty cringe.
